note: Step-free access throughout with accessible tours, lifts and accessible amenities; the wide smooth forecourt is great for rolling and photos. Book accessible tour spots ahead.

The bridge and Pylon Lookout involve stairs, but BridgeClimb can support some mobility needs (ask ahead) and you get great harbour views from the accessible foreshore at Circular Quay / Dawes Point without climbing.

Over 95% of exhibits wheelchair-viewable, elevators/ramps, accessible ferry from Circular Quay + free accessible shuttle; the Sky Safari cable car takes MANUAL chairs (width limits, not electric). Site is hilly - come with a charged chair/companion. Free companion card entry.
Modern precinct completely free of steps; level access to SEA LIFE Aquarium, WILD LIFE Zoo, Madame Tussauds (all step-free with lifts) plus waterfront dining. A top family pick.
74-acre harbourside garden right by the Opera House; wide smooth flat/gently-sloping paths, roll right to the water's edge for skyline views. Free and lovely for families.
Accessible parking, an accessible promenade and lookouts, plus beach matting and beach wheelchairs (book ahead via the Accessible Beaches directory). Bondi Pavilion accessible.
The quay is flat and paved; Sydney's ferries are wheelchair accessible - a ferry ride (e.g. to Manly) is a cheap, scenic accessible way to see the harbour. Note occasional wharf works.
Historic harbourside district with weekend markets and pubs; charming but features cobblestone laneways and uneven terrain and some slopes. Navigable with care.
City-centre observation tower; accessible via lifts with a disabled toilet, and a personalized Skywalk experience can be arranged for wheelchair users in advance. 360 views + revolving restaurant.
Historic ornate shopping arcade; joined to modern malls with lifts and accessible restrooms, more accessible shops/cafes than the nearby Strand Arcade. Good rainy-day stroll.
